---
title: "1. Pre-Class Computing Setup"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

Before class, make sure you have installed R, an R-focused editor, and are able to render a basic Quarto nobook. The below instructions will help you get a headstart on having a working setup.

# Software Requirements

Please set up one of these integrated development environments, or IDEs:

- [RStudio](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop)
- [Positron](https://positron.posit.co/download.html)

You also need:

- [R](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- the R packages listed below, installed from [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- [Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) (RStudio & Positron come bundled with Quarto; you'll only need to look here if Quarto is not already working through RStudio or Positron)


If you are not sure which IDE to choose, use RStudio. It is the most popular R editor and has the most tutorials, answers, and classroom examples available.

Positron is a newer, more modern editor from Posit. RStudio is the safer choice if you are unsure which editor to use.

Either RStudio or Positron will work; under the hood, it's all R.

# Install R

Install R first. R is the programming language itself. RStudio and Positron are editors that use R, but they do not replace R.

Download R here:

<https://cran.r-project.org/>

Choose the installer for your operating system.

- Windows: choose `Download R for Windows`, then install the current base version of R.
- macOS: choose `Download R for macOS`, then install the current `.pkg` file for your computer. 
- Linux: choose your Linux distribution and follow the CRAN instructions.

After installing R, restart RStudio or Positron if either one is already open.

# Install an Editor

## Option 1: RStudio (recommended for beginners)

RStudio Desktop is the safest choice if you are unsure which editor to use.

Download RStudio Desktop here:

<https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop>

Choose the installer for your operating system.

- Windows: download the Windows installer and run it.
- macOS: download the macOS installer, open it, and move RStudio into Applications.
- Linux: download the installer for your Linux distribution.

Open RStudio after installation. You should see an R Console. If RStudio cannot find R, install R first and restart RStudio.

## Option 2: Install Positron

Positron is a new editor built upon Microsoft's VS Code, which is very popular with software developers and data scientists. It is more modern and it feels closer to other modern code editors.

Download Positron here:

<https://positron.posit.co/download.html>

On the download page, check the box to accept the license agreement. The installer links are disabled until you accept the license agreement.

Choose the installer for your operating system.

- Windows: download the Windows `.exe` installer and run it.
- macOS with Apple Silicon: download the `arm64.dmg` file.
- macOS with Intel: download the `x64.dmg` file.
- Linux: download the `.deb` or `.rpm` installer that matches your distribution.

Open Positron after installation. If Positron asks you to choose a runtime or interpreter, choose R.

# Ensure Quarto is Working

Quarto is what turns `.qmd` scientific notebooks into HTML files you can share with your colleagues.

RStudio and Positron ship with their own version of Quarto bundled. I highly recommend using this version. You only need to install Quarto directly if Quarto is missing, out of date, or not working when you try to render.

Should you not be able to get Quarto working, it may help to download Quarto here:

<https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/>

After installing Quarto, restart RStudio or Positron.

# Install Packages

Run this once in the R Console if you do not already have these packages installed:

```{r}
#| eval: false
install.packages(c("dplyr", "ggplot2", "readr", "stringr"))
```

The setup check only uses `dplyr` and `ggplot2`, but installing the full set now will make later work smoother.

# Run The Setup Check

To check your setup, open the setup-check file linked on the course web site. It is named:

```text
02_setup-check.qmd
```

Once you have downloaded the file:

1. Open RStudio or Positron.
2. Open `02_setup-check.qmd`.
3. Run the code chunks one at a time.
4. Click `Render`.
5. Confirm that an HTML file is created.
6. Open the HTML file and confirm that the table and plot appear.

If the setup-check file renders, your basic setup is ready for class.

# If Something Fails

If something does not work, do not panic. Bring the error message to class or email me beforehand [ewestlund@jhu.edu](mailto:ewestlund@jhu.edu) and I can help you out.

The most useful information is:

- your operating system
- whether you are using RStudio or Positron
- the exact error message
- whether the error happened when running a chunk or when rendering
